title: dianthus caryophyllus subsp. longicaulis
intro: Dianthus caryophyllus subsp. longicaulis, also known as sweet William or long-stalked carnation, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the carnation family. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, making it useful in treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Dianthus caryophyllus subsp. longicaulis include reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing, as well as exhibiting potential anticancer and antimicrobial properties. Dianthus caryophyllus subsp. longicaulis can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and its flowers are also used in perfumes and potpourri due to their fragrance and color.
